title: ‘If you protest, you will be shot and killed’: Trump blasts brutal Iranian regime warning
description: US President Donald Trump claims the Iranian regime put out a warning that protesters will be “immediately shot and killed”.  “These people are violent, they killed a minimum of 32,000 people,” Mr Trump said.  “They put out a notice, 2 days ago, if you protest, you will be shot and killed.”

title: Donald Trump blasts Keir Starmer's weak leadership as the UK stalls support of the US
description: US President Donald Trump has criticised UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer as America pushes to secure and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.  Trump specifically slammed Starmer’s inability to decide whether the UK will provide military support to remove Iran’s blockade of the Strait.  “You know, the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom yesterday told me: ‘I’m meeting with my team to make a determination,’” Trump said.  “I said: You don’t need to meet with the team. You’re the Prime Minister; you can make your own (decision). Why do you have to meet with your team to find out whether or not you’re going to send some minesweepers to us or to send some boats?”  Trump previously claimed Starmer put the US and UK’s “special relationship” in jeopardy after refusing to allow American aircraft to land at two military bases.

title: Donald Trump unleashes on NATO allies after abandoning the US during the war with Iran
description: US President Donald Trump lost it at his NATO allies who have let the United States and Israel down in the war effort against Iran.

title: Oscars descend into woke ‘clown show’ as celebrities lose touch
description: Sky News host Danica De Giorgio weighs in on the Oscars, calling the ceremony a “clown show” that exposed just how “out of touch” celebrities have become.  “Roll up, roll up, the clown show arrived in Hollywood today, when a bunch of woke Hollywood elites attended the Oscar Awards,” Ms De Giorgio said.   “It was actually a day to make a political statement on the red carpet, think ICE, Trump derangement syndrome, Palestine, Gaza and Iran.   “Wow, they were lining up in their thousands today to show how out of touch they are with the real world.”

title: Grace Tame’s ‘leftist cult’ cries ‘smear campaign’ amid growing controversy
description: Sky News contributor Calista Clements mocks former Australian of the Year Grace Tame, accusing her of adopting a “victim mentality” amid reports she has lost all remaining speaking engagements for the year.  “A lefty having to take accountability for their actions, who would’ve thought,” Ms Clements told Sky News host Rita Panahi.  “Her leftist cult coming out and saying this is a smear campaign. Now, a smear campaign is when something is false.   “Grace has absolutely made her bed, and she needs to lie in it.”

title: ‘The guy’s out of his depth’: Angus Taylor blasts Bowen over fuel crisis
description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor accuses Energy Minister Chris Bowen of neglecting Australia’s fuel security as the Iran conflict drives up costs.  “Chris Bowen didn't even accept there was a problem as of Tuesday last week, and then by Thursday it was a national crisis. I mean, the guy’s out of his depth,” Mr Taylor said.  “The prime minister needs to take control.”

title: ‘Incompetent government’: Taylor labels fuel shortages a ‘national crisis’
description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor criticises Energy Minister Chris Bowen as Australia faces a “national crisis”, with dozens of petrol stations running out of fuel amid the Iran conflict.  “This energy minister and the government are incompetent, frankly,” Mr Taylor told Sky News Australia.  “He needs to be across where the stocks are, where the holdups are, and in particular where the shortages are.  “He clearly didn't understand that because he was telling us there was no problem.”

title: Trump says he’ll have the ‘honour of taking Cuba’
description: Cuba has been plunged into a nationwide blackout after the country’s electrical grid collapsed on Monday.  The island’s grid operator confirmed the outage on social media, saying investigations are underway to determine the cause.  Around 10 million people have been left without electricity across the country.  US President Donald Trump has since declared he believes he will have “the honour of taking Cuba” in some form.

title: ‘Very scary’: Keir Starmer blasted for running the UK ‘like a dictator’
description: ‘Triggered with Samara Gill’ host Samara Gill blasts UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer for running the country “like a dictator” and leading the country down a “scary path”.   “You’ve got Keir Starmer, our lowly Prime Minister sort of more focused on managing party politics than projecting strength abroad,” Ms Gill told Sky News host Andrew Bolt.   “At the moment, the situation is dire; it really seems like the country is on a knife’s edge right now because we don’t have someone who is uniting people.  “Keir Starmer is running this country a little bit like a dictator, but someone who doesn’t have the conviction of one, and it’s very, very scary the path that this country is going down.  “He’s playing party politics, and he’s actually putting national security at risk in order to secure the Muslim vote that he so desperately needs.”

title: Fury as late Ayatollah compared to the Pope at London rally
description: ‘Triggered with Samara Gill’ host Samara Gill condemns a London protest for promoting “Islamic extremism” and likening the late Ayatollah to the Pope.  “The al-Quds Day protest … was very much about recognising the Ayatollah in all his greatness, many that I spoke to compared the Ayatollah to the Pope and were crying that he was dead,” Ms Gill told Sky News host Andrew Bolt.  “This was very much about sort of touting Islamic extremism, there was death to the IDF chants, there was kill the Jews.   “This was the really ugly side of the movement, and I think that the war in Iran has just perpetuated that … it was extremism.”

title: Taylor urges Australia to back Trump on securing Strait of Hormuz
description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor says the Australian government should support US President Donald Trump in efforts to secure the Strait of Hormuz as oil prices surge during the Iran war.  “We haven't seen any proposal, and we are obviously open to considering any proposal put forward by the government on this," Mr Taylor told Sky News Australia.  “We do want to see the Strait of Hormuz opened up as quickly as possible.  “If we don't, we will continue to see higher prices not just for fuel, but for fertiliser, food and many other products.”

title: Donald Trump unleashes on Ilhan Omar claiming ‘she married her brother supposedly’
description: US President Donald Trump attacked Democrat Ilhan Omar while signing an executive order to establish a task force to combat fraud in America.  Trump specifically attacked the Minnesota Democrats Tim Walz and Ilhan Omar over the Somali community defrauding billions in taxpayer money.   The President mentioned Ilhan Omar will be investigated by the new task force while claiming "she married her brother supposedly".   He said, “Ilhan Omar… she married her brother supposedly.”  “I mean there's a lot of documentation that means she's here illegally and she's a congresswoman, and I hope you're gonna be looking at that or somebody is all right because she's one of the ringleaders here she's bad news, really bad news, she's so bad for our country.”

title: ‘Capacity is tight and airfare is high’: Flight Centre CEO’s warning to Aussie travellers
description: Flight Centre Chief Executive Graham Turner has issued a warning to Australians looking to travel to Europe through the UAE.  This comes after a fuel tank was struck by a drone near Dubai International Airport amid the ongoing war in the Middle East.   “The capacity is going to be tight, and the airfare is going to be relatively high,” Mr Turner told Sky News Australia.  “It’s not obvious how long this is going to last.”

title: JD Vance brutally shuts down reporter for trying to 'drive a wedge' between him and Trump
description: US Vice President JD Vance has ruthlessly shut down a reporter after being questioned about his past views on foreign military interventions.  Vance accused the journalist of "trying to drive a wedge" between himself and US President Donald Trump during an Oval Office press conference.  "You're trying to drive a wedge between members of the administration, between me and the president," Vance said.  "What the president said consistently, going back to 2015, and I agreed with them, is that Iran should not have a nuclear weapon. We have taken this military action under the president's leadership."

title: Trump snaps at ‘obnoxious’ reporter on Air Force One, blasts ABC News as ‘corrupt’
description: US President Donald Trump lashed out at a reporter during a press gaggle aboard Air Force One.  Trump snapped at the journalist, calling them a "very obnoxious person", while fielding questions about the Iran conflict.  The tense exchange came after the reporter pressed the president about troop deployments to the Middle East.  Trump then turned his fire on the reporter's outlet, calling ABC News "one of the most corrupt news organisations."  Trump then declared, "I don't want any more from ABC News."
